**All three are in `src/UsersController.scala`**, embedded directly in what appears to be a hardcoded in-memory list of `User` objects (likely mock/seed data). For example:

```scala
User(1, "Alice", "alice@example.com", LocalDateTime.of(2024, 1, 15, 10, 30)),
User(2, "Bob",   "bob@example.com",   LocalDateTime.of(2024, 2, 20, 14,  0)),
User(3, "Charlie","charlie@example.com",LocalDateTime.of(2024, 3,  5,  9, 15)),
```

**Recommendation:** These hardcoded users (and their email addresses) should be moved to a database, a configuration file, or a test fixture — rather than being baked directly into the application source code.
